content_markdown:
Three identical filament lamps X, Y and Z are connected in a circuit as shown. Each lamp is operating at normal brightness.

The cell has negligible internal resistance. The filament of lamp Y breaks such that its resistance becomes infinite.

Which of the following correctly gives the change in brightness, if any, of the lamps X and Z?

diagram_metadata.description: A circuit diagram showing a DC cell connected in parallel to three identical lamps labeled X, Y, and Z.

options.label: A
options.text: Brightness of lamp X: increases; Brightness of lamp Z: stays the same

options.label: B
options.text: Brightness of lamp X: stays the same; Brightness of lamp Z: stays the same

options.label: C
options.text: Brightness of lamp X: increases; Brightness of lamp Z: increases

options.label: D
options.text: Brightness of lamp X: stays the same; Brightness of lamp Z: increases

vibe_explanation: Since the cell has negligible internal resistance, the potential difference across the parallel branches remains constant and equal to the EMF of the cell. When lamp Y breaks, the voltage across lamps X and Z does not change. Because the resistance of lamps X and Z remains constant, the power dissipated ($P = V^2/R$) and thus the brightness of X and Z remains unchanged.
